figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) S/AM heterostructure with a supercurrent density Js∥x̂, Néel vector n, and proximity-induced pairing gap ∆, and superconducting phase φ. (b) Js-induced staggered NSOT field (BN1,BN2) and 180◦ domain wall with sublattice magnetizations M1, M2 and related n = (M1 −M2)/|M1 − M2|. (c) Js-induced uniaxial anisotropy, described by the free-energy-density difference δFn = Fn − Fx̂ for n = ẑ as a function of the momentum ℏq (blue) and the altermagnetic interaction tm (red), in units of the hopping t, a is the lattice spacing. (d) Similarly for the Js-induced Néel field contribution for n = ŷ. BdG (Ana) label the numerical (analytical) results by lines (crosses). Parameters: µ = 0.05t, tso = 0.2t, tm = 0.2t, ∆0 = 0.2t, and T = 0.1Tc.

Figure 2. (a) The free-energy density obtained from Eq. (2) (labeled BdG), together with the analytical results from Eqs. (8) and (9), for the Néel vector n rotating in the x̂-ŷ plane and (b) in the ẑ-x̂ and ẑ-ŷ planes. (c) The BdG and self-consistent free-energy densities for n rotating in the x̂-ŷ plane and (d) in the ẑ-x̂ plane. Parameters are µ = 0.05t, tso = 0.2t, tm = 0.2t, and ∆ = 0.2t. We use qa = (qa, 0, 0) throughout, with (a) qa = 0.1, (b) 0.01, and (c), (d) 0.2. The self-consistent calculations in (c) and (d) are performed for a system of size Lx × Ly = 30a× 25a with Vs = 5t.

Figure 3. (a) The self-consistent supercurrent, I, for a uniform Néel vector n as a function of the spherical angles θ and ϕ and (b) as a function of ϕ for opposite signs of q at θ = π/2. The same parameters as in Fig. 2 are used. (c) The self-consistent free energy as a function of the domain-wall position r0 as the wall moves through S along x̂ and ŷ, (d) as function of the internal wall angle ϕdw for opposite directions of current. The insets: The domain walls in the calculations. We use δdw = 4a. In (c), (d) Lx = 50a and Ly = 20a are used for a domain wall extending along x̂, and Lx = 20a and Ly = 50a for a domain wall extending along ŷ. The free energy F is referenced to zero for a domain wall at x = 0.2Lx in (c) and at y = 0.2Ly in (d). We define I0 = et/(ℏa). The supercurrent is along x̂ in all panels and |qa| = 2π/50.
